name: Sony WH-1000XM6

specs.Noise Cancellation: HD NC Processor QN3, Adaptive NC Optimizer

whatWorks: Best-in-class noise cancellation — the new QN3 processor with Adaptive NC Optimizer adjusts in real-time to ambient sound and fit, making it noticeably more effective than the XM5 in unpredictable environments.
Sony fixed the XM5's fragile hinge: the XM6 folds flat like the XM4 with visible metal reinforcement, making them genuinely travel-safe in a way the XM5 was not.
37+ hours of ANC battery life — among the longest of any flagship noise-cancelling headphone — and you can now use them while charging via USB-C, which wasn't possible on the XM5.

Sound quality is meaningfully improved: Sony re-engineered the 30mm drivers and smoothed out the XM5's harsh treble peaks, resulting in a warmer, more listenable sound signature that works well without EQ.
worthKnowing: The ear pads feel deflated out of the box compared to the XM5, and the ANC mic protrudes slightly inside the cup, which causes rubbing on larger ears during extended sessions — a real comfort regression from the XM5 for some users.

At ~$600 CAD, the XM6 is a premium purchase. If you already own XM5s in good condition, the upgrade is worthwhile mainly for the improved folding hinge and ANC — not a must-buy if your XM5s are intact.
Multi-point Bluetooth connects to two devices simultaneously, but audio handoff between devices still requires a manual tap in the Sony app — not as seamless as some competitors.

commonComplaints.title: Ear pad comfort has been a recurring topic in r/headphones since launch
commonComplaints.body: Ear pad comfort has been a recurring topic in r/headphones since launch — owners with larger ears specifically report that the ANC mic inside the cup protrudes and causes rubbing during long sessions. The pads also feel noticeably thinner than the XM5 out of the box. Not universal, but consistent enough to plan for.

commonComplaints.title: Multi-device switching still requires a manual tap in the Sony app
commonComplaints.body: Multi-device switching still requires a manual tap in the Sony app — owners coming from AirPods or recent Jabra headphones call this out repeatedly. At $600, automatic handoff is the expectation.

commonComplaints.title: Sony dropped the 3.5mm cable from the box
commonComplaints.body: Sony dropped the 3.5mm cable from the box — this shows up in a large share of Amazon.ca reviews, specifically from frequent flyers who relied on it for airline audio jacks. Small omission, widely noticed.

verdict: Owners who switched from cracked or damaged XM5s say the XM6 is a clear upgrade — the metal-reinforced hinge is the first thing they mention, followed by the ANC improvement. Sound reviews land the same way: the harsh treble that XM5 owners learned to EQ around is noticeably smoother here. The main regression owners flag is ear pad comfort — specifically the ANC mic inside the cup rubbing against larger ears during multi-hour sessions. If your XM5s are intact and the hinge hasn't failed, this is a lateral move. If you're buying new in the category, or your XM5 hinge cracked, this is the one.

realBuyers.quote: The XM5 hinge cracked on mine within a year. The XM6 feels completely different — the metal reinforcement is real and the fold is so much more confidence-inspiring for travel.
Sound is noticeably smoother than the XM5. The harsh treble is gone. These are the first Sonys I've used without immediately going into EQ settings to fix something.
ANC is on another level compared to anything I've used. Put them on in the airport and it's just silence. Worth the price for that alone.

sources.title: SoundGuys: Sony WH-1000XM6 Review — It (mostly) delivers
What Hi-Fi?: Sony WH-1000XM6 review — a sensational performance upgrade

metaDescription: Sony WH-1000XM6 review for Canadian buyers: best-in-class ANC, fixed folding hinge, 37-hour battery, and smoother sound than the XM5. Is it worth $600 CAD?
introText: The Sony WH-1000XM6 are Sony's 2025 flagship noise-cancelling headphones, built around the new HD NC Processor QN3 with 12 microphones and Adaptive NC Optimizer. They address the two biggest complaints about the XM5 — the fragile hinge and the harsh treble — while extending battery life to 37+ hours and adding Bluetooth LE Audio support. Available on Amazon.ca with ASIN B0F3PQHWTZ.
